basics.name: Alireza Yazdiani
basics.label: Ph.D. Candidate in Transportation Engineering
basics.summary: Ph.D. candidate in Transportation Engineering at Cornell University with experience in transportation data science, machine learning, and optimization for public-sector decision-making. Develops Python-based analytical pipelines, predictive and statistical models, and MILP/MINLP optimization frameworks for large-scale mobility, logistics, and environmental planning problems.

work.name: Cornell University
highlights: Spatiotemporal machine learning for PM2.5 hotspot analysis (93% accuracy with XGBoost)
MILP/MINLP optimization for hydrogen refueling station siting and urban air mobility scheduling
ETL pipelines and cloud-based analytics for NYC transportation and emissions modeling

work.name: Cornell University
work.summary: Graduate Courses, Civil and Environmental Engineering
highlights: Six Sigma Design for Reliable Systems, Systems Architecture, Project Management, Economics and Finance

work.name: Sharif University of Technology
work.summary: Graduate Courses, Department of Civil Engineering
highlights: Operation Research, Transportation System Analysis, Simulation

work.name: University of Tehran
work.summary: Undergraduate Courses, Department of Civil Engineering

volunteer.summary: Contributing to cloud-based transportation and environment analysis platform for New York City.

highlights: Research: Transportation data science, machine learning, optimization
Ranked 1st among all M.Sc. students in Transportation Engineering
Ranked within the top 20% of graduating students

awards.title: Student Presentation Competition Certificate

publications.name: Integrated Optimization of Hydrogen Refueling Stations with Green and Blue Hydrogen Production for Freight Corridors
publications.summary: Optimization framework for siting hydrogen refueling infrastructure along freight corridors.

publications.name: An optimization framework for the time-dependent electric vehicle routing problem with shared mobility: a step toward smart cities
publications.summary: Time-dependent EV routing optimization integrating shared mobility for smart city applications.

publications.name: Machine learning in spatiotemporal PM2.5 hotspots analysis for better targeted mitigation strategies
publications.summary: XGBoost-based models achieving 93% accuracy for identifying persistent PM2.5 hotspots.

publications.name: Digitalizing railway operations: An optimization-based train rescheduling model for urban and interurban disrupted networks
publications.summary: Integer programming model for urban rail rescheduling achieving 88% reduction in computational time.

publications.name: AI-Driven Adaptive Air Transit with Modular Aerial Pods
publications.summary: MINLP model for dynamic urban air mobility scheduling with AI-driven demand forecasting.

projects.name: TEAM-NYC: Transportation and Environment Analysis Model
projects.summary: Cloud-based emissions modeling platform using Python, MOVES 5, and ETL pipelines to integrate link-level traffic data across NYC's nine-county region for policy scenario analysis.

projects.name: CMP-NYC: Congestion Management Process Platform
projects.summary: Interactive web-based platform for congestion management integrating data from the New York Best Practice Model with queue-augmented speed-flow engine.
